items.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick takes a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of review
items.summary_auto: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday criticized the current iteration of North America's free trade agreement as a bad deal, taking a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of upcoming negotiations.

items.title: Outpouring of sympathy at visitation for Quebec pilot killed in N.Y. crash
items.summary_auto: People gathered in Coteau-du-Lac, Que., on Friday to remember Captain Antoine Forest. The 30-year-old was one of two Air Canada pilots killed in March when their aircraft collided with a fire truck on the runway of a New York airport.

items.title: Prince George Cougars fined $1,500 after rowdy crowd disrupts game, throws beer onto ice
items.summary_auto: The Prince George Cougars failed to provide adequate security during a chaotic game against the Penticton Vees, Western Hockey League officials say.

items.title: Baby dead, toddler missing after house fire in Brazeau County: RCMP
items.summary_auto: A baby is dead and a toddler is still missing after they were unable to be rescued from a house that caught fire in Brazeau County, police said.

items.title: Baby dead, toddler missing after house fire southwest of Edmonton: RCMP
items.summary_auto: A baby is dead and a toddler is still missing after they were unable to be rescued from a house that caught fire in Brazeau County, police said.

items.title: Stanley Cup playoffs primer: What to know for each first-round series
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter gets you ready for all eight matchups in the opening round of the NHL playoffs, featuring three Canadian teams.

items.title: Has looksmaxxing guru Clavicular gone too far?
items.summary_auto: Braden Peters, also known as Clavicular online, was recently hospitalized after a suspected overdose. The 20-year-old influencer’s content, based around ‘looksmaxxing’ tips, has garnered attention for how extreme and dangerous it can be.

items.title: 'She'll never be seen': Dean Penney tells involvement in estranged wife's death to fictitious crime boss
items.summary_auto: On Nov. 30, 2023, Dean Penney told a fictitious crime boss of his involvement in the death of his estranged wife, Jennifer Hillier-Penney, exactly seven years prior —  and the steps he took to hide her body.

items.title: Crawfish ice cream has become this Texas shop's No. 1 menu item
items.summary_auto: The ice cream — flavoured with cajun seasoning, butter and garlic and topped with an entire crawfish — sounds like a novelty treat. But in the seven years this Texas shop has been serving it up, it's become a staple.

items.title: Ex-UCP candidate who says she was branded a racist hopes 'truth can be aired in full' at trial
items.summary_auto: Caylan Ford is hoping a Calgary courtroom will deliver her justice seven years after her private Facebook messages were leaked to media outlets, a move she says branded her a white supremacist and devastated her life.

items.title: Fact check: Old video does not show U.S. naval blockade of Iran
items.summary_auto: This video has been posted online with false claims that it shows the U.S. naval blockade of Iran. In fact, it's an old video — posted as early as May 2022.

items.title: Ford recalls 144,000 F-150s in Canada due to gearshift issue
items.summary_auto: Safety issue impacts some vehicles with six-speed automatic transmissions made between 2015 and 2017. The issue can cause unexpected downshifting while the vehicle is moving.

items.title: Boeing announces $36M for research and development at Winnipeg facility
items.summary_auto: Boeing plans to spend $36 million on composite aerospace manufacturing research and development projects at its Winnipeg facility, the aerospace company's president announced Friday.

items.title: Canadian mould makers say quiet U.S. tariff tweak loudly impacts their bottom line
items.summary_auto: The new tax levy that came in Apr. 2 applies to the full customs value of metal articles.

items.title: Tegan Quin wins Canada Reads 2026 championing The Cure for Drowning by Loghan Paylor
items.summary_auto: The historical fiction novel won in a 4-1 vote on the final day of the great Canadian book debate.

items.title: Sexual assault charges stayed against Alberta spiritual leader, wife
items.summary_auto: Sexual assault charges against a self-styled spiritual leader and his wife in the Edmonton area have been stayed.

items.title: New B.C. program to 'track and trace' origins of illicit drug supply
items.summary_auto: B.C. officials have announced a new "track and trace" pilot program using technology they say can reveal patterns about the geographic origins and patterns of the illicit drug supply.

items.title: Calls grow for Starmer to resign, as the shadow of Epstein still looms large over 10 Downing St.
items.summary_auto: Calls are growing for Keir Starmer to resign after it was revealed that Peter Mandelson, who was a close friend to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, failed a security vetting process but was appointed as the U.K's ambassador to the U. S. anyway.

items.title: Celine Dion drops new song Dansons ahead of Paris residency
items.summary_auto: Canadian icon Celine Dion dropped a new single Friday, marking her latest step back into the spotlight. The soft, romantic French-language ballad is called Dansons, meaning "Let’s dance" in English.

items.title: Alberta commits to restore power line with Montana to capacity, potentially easing trade tension
items.summary_auto: Alberta has mandated a goal to restore the Montana-Alberta intertie to full capacity, whenever reasonable, potentially providing some relief to a trade irritant cited by U.S. President Donald Trump’s top trade representative last year.

items.title: Acting ICE director to leave as controversies, funding questions continue to swirl around agency
items.summary_auto: Todd Lyons, acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), is planning to depart at the end of next month. Lyons's boss Markwayne Mullin at the Department of Homeland Security says he hopes that the Trump administration can carry out deportations "in a more quiet way," after several deaths and a drumbeat of negative media coverage.

items.title: One-time GST top-up to land in Canadians' accounts in June, Grocery Benefit in July
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government's groceries benefit and GST top-up announced in January will begin landing in Canadians' bank accounts in June.

items.title: Air Canada suspends 6 routes 'no longer economically feasible' amid jet fuel cost crisis
items.summary_auto: Air Canada has suspended service on six different routes, both domestic and cross-border, as the war in the Middle East drives up fuel costs.

items.title: Scientists thrilled as an elusive Greenland shark washes up in Ireland
items.summary_auto: When Dublin scientist Emma Murphy got word that a dead Greenland shark had washed up on the shores of northwestern Ireland, she was gobsmacked. Not only are the massive and ancient ocean dwellers notoriously elusive, but they usually make their homes in the remote depths of the Arctic and North Atlantic oceans.

items.title: Canadian, US stocks rally and oil falls around 9% after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is open
items.summary_auto: Oil prices dropped back to where they were in the early days of the Iran war and U.S. stocks raced to another record Friday, after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open again for commercial tankers carrying oil from the Persian Gulf to customers worldwide.

items.title: After testicular cancer surgery, Forge FC's Kyle Bekker wants men to 'listen to their body'
items.summary_auto: Forge FC midfielder Kyle Bekker gathered recently with teammates to inform them about his recent absence from the Canadian Premier League club. "I had surgery four weeks ago [this past] Monday to remove a tumour after being diagnosed with testicular cancer," Bekker revealed Thursday to CBC Sports.

items.title: Ontario government buys $28.9M private jet for Doug Ford's use
items.summary_auto: The provincial government has purchased a pre-owned private jet for $28.9 million to be used by Ontario Premier Doug Ford.

items.title: Video of woman collapsing in ER waiting room played at Ontario inquest into her sepsis death
McLaren driver charged after supercar slams into wall, bike rack near Toronto's Casa Loma
items.summary_auto: Toronto police said one person has been arrested for impaired driving after a supercar crashed and was stuck propped up against a wall outside Casa Loma.

items.title: Stellantis, Microsoft sign 5-year partnership for AI push
items.summary_auto: The legacy automaker eyes 60 per cent data centre footprint cut by 2029 as it leans into big tech in software, AI Race. Deal is set to co-develop 100+ initiatives at Stellantis.

items.title: Bankruptcy bid adds to financial woes threatening debt-ridden B.C. developer
items.summary_auto: A B.C. developer who has already risked jail time for failing to come clean about the finances behind her lavish lifestyle now faces a possible bankruptcy order over a $74-million loan she guaranteed in relation to prime property in the heart of Vancouver's Cambie Corridor.

items.title: RCMP officer failed to find gun during search of youth being placed in Thompson, Man. detachment cell
items.summary_auto: Police say something "went wrong" when a youth managed to get a gun into a cell at the Thompson RCMP detachment after a search failed to find it on him. The incident happened in 2024. Cleaning staff found the gun behind a toilet two days later, and ammunition was later found in the toilet bowl.

items.title: From political rump to anti-separatism powerhouse: Inside a politico's bid to transform the Alberta Liberals
items.summary_auto: Veteran operative Stephen Carter wants to change the small party into a big referendum advertiser. He may change its name, too.

items.title: Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney has sent invitations to 100 of the biggest investment firms in the world. He's pitching an Invest in Canada Summit this fall to unlock trillions of dollars in investments in Canada.

items.title: 1 killed in Israeli strike in southern Lebanon on 1st full day of ceasefire deal
items.summary_auto: An Israeli drone strike killed one person in southern Lebanon on Friday, the first full day of a U.S.-brokered truce meant to end hostilities between Israel and the militant group Hezbollah, paramedics in the area and the head of a local hospital told Reuters.

items.title: Threats, corruption and intimidation: Is the Bishnoi gang calling shots at Cricket Canada?
'I will be 41.' Teens wonder why it's going to take the city 25 years to build Hamilton, Ont. basketball court
items.summary_auto: Two Hamilton teens are wondering why the city's plan to build one much needed basketball court in Gage Park is going to take 25 years.

items.title: Court weighs overturning rule blocking birth alert class action from suing 49 children’s aid societies
items.summary_auto: The Ontario Court of Appeal will now decide whether pregnant mothers targeted by controversial birth alerts — a practice that led to newborns being taken away from their families — can pursue a class-action lawsuit against 49 children's aid societies across the province.

items.title: Canadian firm ignored worker complaints over drug cartel-management links at Mexican gold mine, sources say
items.summary_auto: Several workers for a Canadian-owned Mexican gold mine received no response after making complaints to the company’s workplace ethics hotline saying that local management was working with a prominent organized crime group to force out the existing union, according to two former local union members.

items.title: Oil and gas emissions rising as other sectors mostly flat, federal data shows
items.summary_auto: Progress on cutting carbon emissions has stalled somewhat in Canada, according to the federal government annual reporting to the UN. Oil and gas emissions have continued to rise since the pandemic.

items.title: A chimpanzee 'civil war' erupted in Uganda. Scientists aren't sure why
items.summary_auto: An unusually large troop of chimpanzees displayed cooperation and social cohesion for decades, until something shifted and they started to turn on each other.

items.title: Why is Lee Cronin's The Mummy called that? Also, why is it so terrible?
items.summary_auto: Lee Cronin's The Mummy, a needlessly mean-spirited horror flick that's barely about mummies, may have you asking a million questions. And before you even get to the plot-related ones, the most obvious is: what's with that title?

items.title: 'It's like I gave birth to twins': Canadian director Chandler Levack has 2 movies opening on the same day
items.summary_auto: In Montreal, a young woman in a Spin magazine T-shirt stares furiously at the blank page on her 2011 MacBook. It's a moment from the new movie Mile End Kicks that could have been ripped from director Chandler Levack's own life, when the former critic traded Toronto for Montreal's exploding music scene. Mile End Kicks is one of two movies from Levack opening on Friday.
